title: Coconut Hearty French Pork Stir-fry
description: A comforting French-inspired stirfry that marries tender pork with coconut flakes and savory herbs, all while keeping sodium low for a cozy, hearty meal.
instructions: Whisk low‑sodium soy sauce, Dijon mustard, lemon juice, and black pepper to make the seasoning sauce.
Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a large skillet over medium‑high heat. Add pork slices; brown on all sides, about 4‑5 minutes. Transfer pork to a plate.
In the same skillet, add remaining tablespoon olive oil. Add sliced onion and bell pepper; cook 3 minutes until softened.
Add minced garlic and fresh thyme; sauté 30 seconds until fragrant.
Return pork to the skillet; pour the whisked sauce over and toss to coat.
Sprinkle coconut flakes over the stir‑fry; cook 2‑3 minutes, stirring, until flakes begin to toast slightly.
Adjust seasoning if needed; remove from heat.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped parsley. Pair with steamed green beans or a small portion of brown rice if desired.
